hero.title: About Q4S
hero.subtitle: Quality Force — our foundation, our promise.

story.title: Our Story
story.body:
Q4S B.V. was founded from a passion for quality and a deep understanding of the technical industry. As a specialised recruitment and project partner agency, we connect highly qualified technical professionals with the most complex industrial and infrastructure projects in the Netherlands and beyond.

Our name stands for Quality Force — a philosophy that resonates in everything we do. We believe that the right person in the right place makes the difference between a good project and an outstanding one.

mission.title: Mission & Vision
mission.mission: We connect highly qualified technical professionals with critical projects, with quality, compliance and performance at the centre.
mission.vision: We strive to be the most trusted partner for technical recruitment and project support in the industrial sector.

items.title: Quality
items.desc: We deliver no compromises. Every professional we place meets the highest standards.

items.desc: In-depth knowledge of QA/QC, inspection and technical recruitment is our strength.

team.title: Our Approach
team.body: The Q4S team consists of experienced professionals with a background in the technical industry. We speak the language of our clients and our candidates. That makes the difference.

hero.title: Way We Work
hero.subtitle: Transparent, efficient and always focused on the best result.

wayWeWork.intro: At Q4S we believe in a clear and transparent approach. From first contact to successful placement — we keep you informed at every step.

steps.title: Intake & Briefing
steps.desc: We start with a comprehensive intake meeting to fully understand your project, organisation and exact requirements. No assumptions — just facts.

steps.title: Selection & Screening
steps.desc: Based on your briefing, we select the most suitable candidates from our network. Every candidate is thoroughly screened on technical knowledge, work experience and references.

steps.title: Presentation
steps.desc: We present you with a shortlist of the best candidates, complete with comprehensive profiles and our own assessment. You decide who you want to meet.

steps.title: Matching & Placement
steps.desc: After the interviews, we guide the selection process and ensure smooth contract handling. The professional starts ready to immediately contribute to your project.

steps.title: Support & Follow-up
steps.desc: During and after the placement, we remain involved. We monitor progress and are the first point of contact for both client and professional.

steps.title: Register & Upload CV
steps.desc: Create a profile and upload your CV. Our recruiters will contact you as soon as a suitable assignment is available.

steps.title: Introduction Meeting
steps.desc: We get to know you — your expertise, ambitions and wishes. This allows us to match you optimally.

steps.title: Matching
steps.desc: We link your profile to suitable assignments. We keep you actively informed of new opportunities.

steps.title: Introduction & Start
steps.desc: After a successful match, we ensure a smooth introduction at the client and a good start.

items: Access to exclusive assignments in the industrial sector

hero.title: NDT — Non-Destructive Testing
hero.subtitle: Specialist NDT talent for the most demanding industrial environments.

ndt.intro: Non-Destructive Testing is a critical discipline in industrial quality surveillance. Q4S delivers certified NDT inspectors and consultants who can perform the most complex inspections — from offshore platforms to petrochemical installations.

items.title: Ultrasonic Testing
items.desc: Detection of internal flaws in materials using sound waves.

items.title: Radiographic Testing
items.desc: X-ray or gamma radiation for detecting internal defects.

items.title: Magnetic Particle Testing
items.desc: Detection of surface and near-surface defects in ferromagnetic materials.

items.title: Liquid Penetrant Testing
items.desc: Detection of surface defects using liquid penetrants.

items.title: Eddy Current Testing
items.desc: Detection of defects in conductive materials using electromagnetic induction.

items.title: Visual Inspection
items.desc: Systematic assessment of materials, joints and structures.

expertise.title: Our NDT Expertise
items: Level II & III certified inspectors (CSWIP, PCN, ASNT)
Weld inspection and welding coordination (IWI, CWI)
Procedure review and weld procedure qualification (WPS/PQR)
System audits and NDT procedure verification
Inspections per ASME, EN, API, ISO standards
Support for offshore, onshore and petrochemical projects

cta.title: Looking for an NDT specialist?
cta.body: Contact Q4S and we will deliver the right certified professional for your project.

hero.title: News & Insights
hero.subtitle: Industry updates, Q4S news and insights from our experts.

articles.title: Growing demand for NDT inspectors in the energy sector
articles.excerpt: The energy transition is driving increasing demand for qualified NDT inspectors. Q4S sees this trend and responds with a growing network of certified professionals.
articles.content:
The energy transition is one of the key drivers behind the growing demand for NDT inspectors. Wind farms, solar plants and hydrogen installations require stringent quality controls, and demand for certified NDT professionals now exceeds supply.

Q4S has been tracking this trend for several years. Our network of certified NDT inspectors grew by more than 30% in 2025. Yet the market for specialised technical talent remains tight — particularly for specialists with Level III certifications in UT and RT.

**Why demand is increasing**

The energy transition is bringing new projects online, but ageing infrastructure also requires more frequent inspection. Stricter EU regulations are forcing companies to inspect more often and more thoroughly. At the same time, a wave of retiring inspectors has created a significant gap in supply.

**What this means for clients**

Companies that rely on NDT inspectors for project planning are advised to engage early with a specialist agency like Q4S. We recommend securing NDT resources at least 8 weeks before project start.

Q4S is happy to help you find the right NDT specialist for your project. Contact us for a no-obligation consultation.

articles.title: Q4S strengthens presence in the offshore market
articles.excerpt: Q4S has expanded its network with more than 50 certified offshore inspectors. This allows us to respond quickly and effectively to assignments in the North Sea region.
articles.content:
Q4S has significantly strengthened its position in the offshore sector over the past 12 months. With the addition of more than 50 certified offshore inspectors to our network, we are better placed than ever to respond quickly and flexibly to assignments in the North Sea region and beyond.

**New partnerships**

Alongside expanding our freelance network, we have formed strategic partnerships with several Tier 1 contractors active in the UK and Norwegian offshore sectors. This enables us to deliver the right professionals quickly for international assignments too.

**Focus on certified talent**

Q4S screens all offshore professionals for valid offshore certificates (BOSIET, HUET, OPITO) and medical clearances. We now maintain a pool of more than 80 offshore-qualified inspectors across QA/QC, NDT and Source Inspection.

**Future plans**

In 2026, Q4S will further expand its activities into the Scandinavian market. We welcome contacts with new clients and candidates in this segment. If you are active in offshore and looking for technical talent, please get in touch.

articles.title: Why source inspection is crucial for your supply chain
articles.excerpt: Source inspection prevents costly delays and quality issues. In this article, our experts explain why third-party inspection is an indispensable part of your supplier management.
articles.content:
In complex industrial projects, the quality of components and materials is fundamental to the success of the final result. Source inspection — inspecting goods at the supplier's premises before shipment — is one of the most effective tools for intercepting quality issues early.

**What is source inspection?**

Source inspection (also known as third-party inspection or vendor inspection) is the process whereby an independent inspector assesses the fabrication quality of ordered goods at the supplier on behalf of the client. This can apply to pressure vessels, piping, electrical equipment, steel structures and virtually any other industrial component.

**The benefits**

The benefits of source inspection are numerous:

- **Early problem detection**: Deviations are discovered before shipment, not upon receipt — saving significant rework and delay costs.
- **Independent verification**: A third party has no interest in overlooking defects.
- **Supplier management**: Suppliers perform better when they know independent oversight is in place.
- **Documentation**: The inspector produces detailed reports that make the quality chain demonstrable for certifying authorities.

**When to use source inspection?**

Source inspection is particularly valuable for critical components, long lead times, first-time suppliers, or projects where non-conformities are especially costly.

Q4S maintains a worldwide network of source inspectors with in-depth knowledge of industrial standards. Our inspectors are active in Europe, Asia, the Middle East and North America. Contact us for more information about our source inspection services.

articles.title: Q4S welcomes new recruiter specialised in QA/QC
articles.excerpt: To optimally serve our growing client portfolio, Q4S has appointed a new recruiter with over 10 years of experience in technical recruitment for the industrial sector.
articles.content:
Q4S is growing, and we are pleased to announce that we have strengthened our team with a new senior recruiter. With over 10 years of experience in technical recruitment for the QA/QC and inspection sector, our new colleague brings a broad network and deep domain expertise.

**Expanding capacity**

Growth in our client portfolio called for additional recruitment capacity. With our new recruiter on board, we can respond even faster and more carefully to client requests — including for urgent and complex vacancies.

**Expertise in QA/QC and NDT**

Our new team member specialises in recruiting QA/QC Engineers, Welding Inspectors, NDT specialists and Source Inspectors. Thanks to her extensive network in the sector, we expect to further reduce placement times and improve the quality of our matches.

Are you looking for technical talent or a new challenge? We would love to hear from you. Email info@q4s.nl or call us on +31 (0) 85 7826818.

articles.title: Trends in technical recruitment 2026: what to expect
articles.excerpt: The labour market for technical professionals remains tight. We analyse the key trends and offer practical advice for both clients and candidates.
articles.content:
2026 promises to be another challenging year in the labour market for technical professionals. Q4S shares its observations and advice for both clients and candidates.

**Trend 1: Growing scarcity in specialised roles**

Demand for QA/QC inspectors, NDT specialists and Source Inspectors has exceeded supply for several years. In 2026 we expect this trend to continue, partly due to the start of major infrastructure projects and energy transition investments. Clients who start recruiting early have a considerable advantage.

**Trend 2: Internationalisation of the labour market**

More and more technical professionals are willing to work on assignments abroad. Q4S is seeing growing interest in assignments in Scandinavia, the Middle East and Asia. For clients, this opens doors to a broader talent pool.

**Trend 3: Importance of certifications**

Clients are setting ever-higher requirements for certifications. CSWIP, PCN, IWI and ASNT Level III certificates have become virtually mandatory for senior positions. Candidates who invest in their certifications have a clear competitive advantage.

**Advice for clients**

Start your recruitment process early. Work with a specialist agency that has access to a broad, certified candidate network. Q4S is ready to support you.

**Advice for candidates**

Keep your certifications current and be open to international assignments. A strong CV with demonstrable project experience in heavy industry remains the best investment.

articles.title: The importance of certified weld inspectors on major projects
articles.excerpt: Weld joints are critical components in infrastructure and industrial projects. Discover why certified weld inspectors are indispensable.
articles.content:
Weld joints are the backbone of industrial structures. Whether it is pipelines, pressure vessels, offshore platforms or bridges — the quality of the welds largely determines the safety and service life of the installation. Certified weld inspectors play a crucial role in this.

**What does a weld inspector do?**

A Welding Inspector (also known as Welding Quality Inspector) oversees all welding-related activities on a project. This includes:

- **Pre-qualification**: Verification of welding procedures (WPS) and qualification certificates (PQR, WQT)
- **In-process inspection**: Checks on preheat, interpass temperature, weld sequence and consumables used
- **Post-weld inspection**: Visual inspection per EN 5817 or AWS D1.1, followed by NDT if required
- **Documentation**: Maintaining weld registers, weld maps and inspection reports

**Certified vs. non-certified**

The difference between a certified and non-certified weld inspector can be significant. Certifications such as IWI (International Welding Inspector) from the IIW or CWI (Certified Welding Inspector) from the AWS guarantee that the inspector has sufficient technical knowledge and is up to date with the latest standards.

For major industrial and infrastructure projects, a certified weld inspector is in many cases contractually required. Q4S has an extensive network of certified Welding Inspectors with experience across a range of sectors. Contact us for more information.
